title: SAIL app-instance pool registry
description: This schema defines the runtime cache of pooled app-under-test instances, their checkouts, and the FIFO wait queue that SAIL (Sweetspot App Instance Lease) maintains. Pool tooling and Gen3 dashboards read it to answer which instance is leased, warm, dirty, or waiting. Read it before consuming registry/sail-instances.generated.json. Remember the file is regenerated on every pool mutation and must never be hand-edited.

x-sma-contract.change_policy: Optional additive fields preserve v1 compatibility; required, renamed, or narrowed fields require a new versioned $id.
x-sma-contract.rollback: The registry is a reconstructible runtime cache: deleting it loses only warm-instance bookkeeping, and the next prune rebuilds records from live processes and leases.
x-sma-contract.lifecycle: Created and regenerated by the listed writer under the registry lock; validated at its consumer boundary.
x-sma-contract.retention: Runtime cache; intentionally git-ignored like active-leases.generated.json.
x-sma-contract.performance: Static file contract; validation performance is covered by the sail selftest.

instances.description: Every instance the pool currently owns, in no particular order.

instance_id.description: Stable identity of the pooled instance.

pid.description: Process id; null only while LAUNCHING.

start_token.description: Platform-opaque start token; pid alone is never identity.

cdp.description: CDP HTTP endpoint handed to the lease holder.

fingerprint.description: Build fingerprint this instance is running; reuse requires an exact match.

dirty.description: True when state was mutated or the previous checkout expired; dirty instances recycle instead of reuse.

generation.description: Monotonic checkout counter; the fencing token component.

leases_served.description: Total checkouts served, for jittered recycling policies.

pool_lease_id.description: Pool-ownership lease that SPL registration hangs off.

hud_pid.description: HUD keeper process id, when the HUD is enabled.

hud.description: What the in-window HUD currently shows.

last_activity.description: ISO-8601 time of the last checkout, release, renewal, or HUD update.

checkout.description: The live checkout, or null while IDLE.

queue.description: FIFO wait tickets; served strictly head-first per project.

expires_at.description: Ticket lifetime bound; expired tickets are pruned, never served.

events.description: Bounded rolling log of pool transitions for doctor output and dashboards.
